parsed.answer: When a user types `claude` and presses Enter, Claude Code launches and displays a welcome/startup screen in the terminal. The screen is enclosed in a bordered box drawn with Unicode box-drawing characters. Inside the box, there is a two-column layout. The left side contains a small ASCII art logo (resembling the Anthropic/Claude logo using Unicode block characters or slashes/backslashes), version information, and possibly the model name being used. The right side contains tips or getting-started information, such as keyboard shortcuts and basic usage hints. Below the box, there is an input prompt (typically '>' or similar) where the user can type their first message or command. The overall appearance is a structured, text-based UI rendered entirely in the terminal using ANSI/Unicode characters.

claims.statement: The startup screen is enclosed in a bordered box using Unicode box-drawing characters
The layout inside the box is two-column
The left side contains an ASCII/Unicode art logo
The left side contains version information
The right side contains tips or getting-started information
There is an input prompt below the box for user input

parsed.uncertainty_notes: I have limited reliable knowledge of the exact visual appearance of the Claude Code CLI startup screen.
